work_statement_unknown.head: Couldn't confirm from the extracted text
work_statement_unknown.reason: The extracted text contains CLINs for a WF Fixture Assembly (F-16 test equipment, NSN 4920-01-537-5649) and a Counterfeit Prevention Plan CLIN, but no Statement of Work, PWS, or SOO text was extracted. The governing work statement is almost certainly in an un-parsed attachment or separate document not captured in the extraction.
work_statement_unknown.action: Upload the attachment to resolve this. It's the single highest-leverage line in the audit — it decides whether you propose a method (SOW) or propose to outcomes (PWS/SOO).

l02_catches.title: Wrong WAWF Invoice Type for NSN Supply Items Will Cause Payment Rejection
l02_catches.why_invisible: For NSN-based supply contracts, WAWF requires a specific combination of invoice and receiving report document types (typically 2-in-1 or combo). Contractors frequently submit a standalone invoice without the required receiving report, which passes WAWF system validation but is rejected by the paying office, delaying payment by 30-60 days on an FFP contract.
l02_catches.move: Confirm with the contracting officer and DFAS the required WAWF document type combination for CLIN 0001 before first delivery. Set up the WAWF routing codes (DoDAAC for acceptor, paying office, and contract administrator) in advance of first shipment.

l02_catches.title: JCP Certification Lead Time for Export-Controlled F-16 Technical Data Can Exceed 60 Days
l02_catches.why_invisible: The NSN 4920-01-537-5649 is for F-16 test equipment, which almost certainly involves export-controlled technical data requiring Joint Certification Program (JCP) access through DTIC. This requirement is not stated in the extracted clauses but is enforced at the data repository level — the contractor discovers it only when attempting to download drawings, after proposal submission.
l02_catches.move: Before proposal submission, contact the contracting officer to confirm TDP availability and whether JCP certification is required. If JCP is needed, initiate the certification application immediately — do not wait for award.

l02_catches.title: Counterfeit Prevention Plan Must Be Submitted as a Deliverable, Not Just Maintained Internally
l02_catches.why_invisible: CLIN 0002 is NSP (Not Separately Priced) and associated with document FD20202600454, which likely specifies a Contract Data Requirements List (CDRL) submission. Contractors often treat the Counterfeit Prevention Plan as an internal document and miss the formal CDRL submission deadline, triggering a contract deficiency and potentially delaying acceptance of CLIN 0001 deliverables.
l02_catches.move: Obtain document FD20202600454 and identify the CDRL submission date for the Counterfeit Prevention Plan. Schedule the plan development and government review cycle to ensure submission occurs before or concurrent with first article/first delivery.

l02_catches.title: FOB Point Unknown — Packaging, Crating, and Transportation Costs May Be Unpriced in FFP
l02_catches.why_invisible: No delivery schedule or FOB terms were extracted. For F-16 test equipment (potentially large, heavy, or fragile fixtures), packaging to MIL-STD-2073 and transportation costs can be substantial. If FOB is Origin, the government arranges transportation but the contractor bears packaging costs. If FOB is Destination, the contractor bears all transportation costs. Misidentifying the FOB point in an FFP leads to unrecoverable cost overruns.
l02_catches.move: Obtain the complete solicitation and identify the FOB point and packaging requirements (MIL-STD-2073 applicability). Price packaging and transportation costs explicitly in the FFP build-up, and confirm with the contracting officer before submission.

l02_catches.title: F-16 End-Item Delivery or Testing at a Military Installation Requires Base Access Credentials with 30-90 Day Lead Time
l02_catches.why_invisible: The end item is F-16 (likely Lockheed Martin or Air Force depot), and delivery or acceptance testing may occur at a military installation. Base access for contractor personnel (Common Access Card or visitor credentials) requires background checks and sponsorship that can take 30-90 days. This is not mentioned in any extracted clause but is enforced at the installation level and can delay acceptance.
l02_catches.move: Identify the delivery/acceptance location from the work statement and contact the installation security office immediately after award to initiate base access requests. Factor this lead time into the delivery schedule risk assessment during proposal preparation.

l02_catches.title: 52.204-24 Telecommunications Representation Must Match BOM — Inconsistency Creates False Certification Risk
l02_catches.why_invisible: The 52.204-24 representation is a mandatory submission requirement, but it is often completed based on general company knowledge rather than a contract-specific BOM review. If the BOM later reveals a covered telecommunications component, the representation becomes a false certification — a potential False Claims Act violation — even if the error was unintentional.
l02_catches.move: Complete the 52.204-24 representation only after conducting a contract-specific BOM review for NSN 4920-01-537-5649. Document the BOM review as the basis for the representation and retain it in the proposal file.

l02_catches.title: Hexavalent Chromium Waiver Process Must Begin Before Proposal Submission If Any Cr6+ Process Is Identified
l02_catches.why_invisible: DFARS 252.223-7008 prohibits Cr6+ but allows waivers for specific applications. The waiver process (DD Form 1694 or equivalent) requires engineering justification, government review, and approval — typically 60-90+ days. Contractors often discover the Cr6+ issue during manufacturing, after award, when it is too late to obtain a waiver without a schedule impact, creating an FFP cost and schedule crisis.
l02_catches.move: Conduct a manufacturing process audit for Cr6+ content before proposal submission. If any Cr6+ process is identified as potentially necessary, initiate the waiver request process immediately and disclose the status to the contracting officer in the proposal.

confidence_notes.uncertain: The issuing office is described as 'other than DoD, NASA, and the Coast Guard,' yet the contract contains multiple DFARS clauses and an AFFARS clause (5352.201-9101), which is contradictory and could not be resolved from the extracted data.
confidence_notes.assumption: It was assumed this may be a DLA or civilian agency action administered on behalf of the Air Force, with DFARS clauses included by reference due to DoD funding.
confidence_notes.resolve: Obtain the solicitation cover page (SF-1449 or DD-1707) and confirm the contracting office DoDAAC, funding agency, and applicable regulatory supplement (FAR only vs. FAR+DFARS+AFFARS).

confidence_notes.uncertain: The offer due date was not extracted and is listed as 'unknown,' making it impossible to confirm whether the proposal deadline is imminent.
confidence_notes.assumption: It was assumed the solicitation is still open and the deadline has not passed, but this cannot be confirmed.
confidence_notes.resolve: Access the original solicitation on SAM.gov using the solicitation number (once obtained) to confirm the exact offer due date, time, and timezone.

confidence_notes.uncertain: The solicitation number was not extracted and is listed as 'unknown,' preventing verification of document completeness, amendment tracking, or contracting officer contact.
confidence_notes.assumption: It was assumed the extracted data represents the current version of the solicitation without amendments, but this cannot be confirmed.
confidence_notes.resolve: Locate the solicitation number on the original document header or SAM.gov posting and verify the current amendment number.

confidence_notes.uncertain: No SOW, PWS, or SOO text was extracted, making it impossible to confirm the scope of work, performance requirements, or technical specifications for the WF Fixture Assembly.
confidence_notes.assumption: It was assumed the work statement exists as an un-parsed attachment and that the contract is for manufacture/delivery of the NSN item per a TDP, based on the CLIN description.
confidence_notes.resolve: Obtain and review the complete solicitation package including all attachments, CDRLs, and referenced documents (especially FD20202600454) from the issuing office or SAM.gov.

confidence_notes.uncertain: Zero delivery line items were extracted, so required delivery dates, FOB point, and shipping instructions are unknown.
confidence_notes.assumption: It was assumed delivery terms exist in an un-parsed section of the solicitation and that standard MIL-STD packaging requirements apply given the NSN and F-16 end item designation.
confidence_notes.resolve: Obtain the complete solicitation Schedule section (Section F) to confirm delivery dates, FOB point, and packaging/marking requirements.

confidence_notes.uncertain: The NAICS code was not extracted and is listed as 'unknown,' preventing verification of small business size standard applicability and competitive landscape assessment.
confidence_notes.assumption: It was assumed the NAICS code is consistent with manufacturing of aircraft test equipment (likely 334511 — Search, Detection, Navigation, Guidance, Aeronautical Systems and Instruments Manufacturing, or 332999 — All Other Miscellaneous Fabricated Metal Product Manufacturing).
confidence_notes.resolve: Obtain the solicitation cover page or SAM.gov posting to confirm the NAICS code and verify the applicable small business size standard.
